basic_details.job_title: BARC Hospital Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 19 PGRMO, Senior Resident Doctor and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) has officially announced the Resident Medical Officer Recruitment 2026 with 19 vacancies on a contractual basis. Candidates not exceeding 40 years of age and possessing MS/MD/DNB qualifications, a Diploma with 2 years of experience, or MBBS with completed internship as per the post requirements are eligible to apply. Interested candidates can attend the walk-in interview scheduled for 09 July 2026. Selected candidates will receive a monthly salary ranging from ₹96,000 to ₹1,20,000 depending on the post and tenure. Candidates should refer to the official

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 40 years for all posts. Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: MS/MD/DNB or Diploma with 2 years experience (for PG posts), MBBS with internship (for Non-DNB JR/SR posts)
qualification.details: Post Graduate Resident Medical Officer/Senior Resident (PG) - All Specialties: MS/MD/DNB degree or Diploma from a recognized university in the concerned specialty. Diploma candidates must possess a minimum of 2 years of post-diploma experience. Degree/Diploma not awarded before December 2022 preferably. Non-DNB Junior/Senior Resident Doctor: Junior: MBBS with one year internship. Senior: MBBS plus PG Diploma or MBBS with one year JR experience preferably in the requisite discipline.

salary_details.details: For PGRMO/SR (PG): ₹1,12,000/- for the 1st year, ₹1,15,000/- for the 2nd year, and ₹1,20,000/- for the 3rd year. For Jr. Resident Doctor (Non-DNB): ₹ 96,000/- per month. For Sr. Resident Doctor (Non-DNB): ₹ 99,000/- per month.

content_sections.heading: BARC Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the BARC Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download BARC Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: BARC Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Post Graduate Resident Medical Officer/Senior Resident (PG) - All Specialties: MS/MD/DNB degree or Diploma from a recognized university in the concerned specialty. Diploma candidates must possess a minimum of 2 years of post-diploma experience. Degree/Diploma not awarded before December 2022 preferably. Non-DNB Junior/Senior Resident Doctor: Junior: MBBS with one year internship. Senior: MBBS plus PG Diploma or MBBS with one year JR experience preferably in the requisite discipline.

content_sections.heading: BARC Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Maximum Age Limit: 40 years for all posts. Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

content_sections.heading: BARC Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Stipend
content_sections.text: For PGRMO/SR (PG): ₹1,12,000/- for the 1st year, ₹1,15,000/- for the 2nd year, and ₹1,20,000/- for the 3rd year. For Jr. Resident Doctor (Non-DNB): ₹ 96,000/- per month. For Sr. Resident Doctor (Non-DNB): ₹ 99,000/- per month.

content_sections.heading: BARC Recruitment 2026 - Walk-In Procedure
content_sections.text: Read the official notification carefully before attending the interview. Candidates must report at the venue as per the reporting time mentioned in the notification. Carry all original documents along with one set of self-attested photocopies such as ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if required), and category certificate (if applicable). Bring the duly filled application form if required in the notification. Verify eligibility criteria before attending the interview. Candidates are advised to reach the venue well in advance to complete registration formalities. Keep extra passport-size photographs for submission if required.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies announced by BARC?
faq.answer: Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) has announced a total of 19 vacancies for the post of Resident Medical Officer.

faq.question: Q2. What is the application mode for BARC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application mode for this recruitment is Walk-in. Interested candidates need to attend the interview directly at the specified venue and time.

faq.question: Q3. What is the walk-in interview date for BARC Resident Medical Officer posts?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the BARC Resident Medical Officer posts is scheduled for 09 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What are the educational qualifications required for the Resident Medical Officer posts?
faq.answer: For PG posts, candidates need MS/MD/DNB or Diploma with 2 years of experience. For Non-DNB JR/SR posts, MBBS with internship is required.

faq.question: Q5. What is the maximum age limit for the BARC Resident Medical Officer positions?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all Resident Medical Officer posts is 40 years.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary offered for the Resident Medical Officer posts?
faq.answer: The salary varies based on the post. For PG posts, it ranges from ₹1,12,000/- to ₹1,20,000/- per month for the first three years. For Non-DNB posts, it is ₹96,000/- for Jr. Resident and ₹99,000/- for Sr. Resident per month.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for BARC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, self-attested photocopies, ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if applicable), and category certificates (if applicable).

faq.question: Q9. Where is the BARC recruitment drive being held?
faq.answer: The recruitment drive is being held at Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) in Mumbai, Maharashtra, Pincode 400094.

faq.question: Q10. What is the selection process for these posts?
faq.answer: The selection process primarily involves a Walk-in Interview. Shortlisting may be done based on MBBS percentage if more than 10 candidates apply for a post.
